response.text: Done — It’s a ~2.50 Gb/s NRZ serial signal with a reasonably open but jitter-limited eye: UI ≈ 400 ps, eye width ≈ 346 ps, total jitter ≈ 54 ps, and jitter is dominated by deterministic/data-dependent jitter (~32–34 ps) rather than random jitter (~1.7 ps). The eye height at the chosen threshold is only about 80 mV, so vertical margin is modest. Overall: the link is working, but signal integrity is mainly limited by deterministic effects such as channel loss/ISI, pattern dependence, or equalization/setup issues—not DDR behavior.
